g.text: Supplementary Figure S1 | Optimiser-boundary diagnostic for all six production clades (Yang & dos Reis 2011): five LRT = 0 clades under the symmetric rule plus the *Apis mellifera* Gr_sweet production-positive clade under the asymmetric rule (6 clades × 5 ω₀ starts = 30 codeml runs). (A) Branch-site Model A LRT magnitude across 5 randomised starting values of ω₀. Three clades (Aedes aegypti, Coleoptera, Lepidoptera) converge to the boundary (LRT = 0, ω₂ₐ = 1.0) at all five starts and are classified TRUE_NULL [NULL]. Two clades (D. melanogaster Gr64 cluster, D. mel. all-Grs) escape the boundary only at ω₀ = 5.0, with LRT = 9.68 and 4.33 respectively, and are classified OPTIMIZER_ARTIFACT [ART.] — orange borders. The Apis Gr_sweet clade reproduces the production basin (LRT = 9.92, ω₂ₐ = 36.18) at 4 of 5 starts but escapes to a sub-null basin at ω₀ = 5.0 (LRT = −2.48, ω₂ₐ = 1.63 — alt model fits worse than null), classifying APIS_OPTIMIZER_ARTIFACT under the asymmetric rule (any near-null escape, LRT < 1.0, on a production-positive clade) — magenta dashed border. The NA cell (D. mel. Gr64 cluster, ω₀ = 2.0) is a 2-h codeml timeout. (B) Recovered foreground ω₂ₐ as a function of ω₀ start. Both dmel escape points fall within the small-clade boundary-artefact range (ω = 30–200; Anisimova & Yang 2007), and the Apis production ω₂ₐ = 36.2 also lies in this range — all three non-zero escapes are consistent with optimiser sensitivity rather than confirmed positive selection.
